docs.title: Holo Privacy
docs.summary: Control what an AI agent may learn about you. You are the data subject; agents acting for you get only the minimum needed for a stated purpose as a signed, verifiable disclosure and never enough to track you.
docs.body: Holo Privacy is the agent-facing companion to Holo Terms. Where Holo Terms governs what an app may touch, Holo Privacy governs what an agent acting on your behalf may disclose so a delegate can be useful without becoming a surveillance channel. It encodes the agentprivacy / blades framework entirely in established W3C privacy vocabulary, so it works with existing internet apps and agents. You are the data subject (W3C Verifiable Credentials holder) reusing your one Holo Terms identity. A delegate agent acts for you but never holds your data; it only ever receives minimal disclosures. Each disclosure is a W3C Verifiable Presentation revealing only the claims a purpose needs (IETF SD-JWT selective disclosure) and can prove a fact (“over 18”) without revealing the value behind it. Standard disclosure policies bind a DPV Purpose to the personal-data categories it may touch, with data-minimisation and no-linkage constraints (W3C ODRL). Every disclosure and consent is a content-addressed, signed record, re-derived + proof-verified before honored (Law L5) enforced once at the agent seam, for every agentic AI framework

docs.title: Holo Terms
docs.summary: Your machine-readable personal terms (MyTerms / IEEE 7012-2025): you propose, each app agrees, the OS enforces. Choose your standing term; see and revoke what every app was granted.
docs.body: Holo Terms encodes MyTerms IEEE 7012-2025, the Standard for Machine Readable Personal Privacy Terms natively in Hologram OS. You are the first party: you propose your terms from the Customer Commons roster. Each app is the second party and must agree before the OS will grant it any capability beyond a bare default-deny sandbox. First-party-proposes: your standing term (default SD-BASE equitable, no surveillance) governs every app. Each app is granted only declared ∩ agreed; sensitive requests (camera, mic, screen, location) and sharing ask you. Every agreement is a content-addressed, signed record (W3C Verifiable Credential), re-derived + proof-verified before any grant (Law L5). Enforced at one OS seam (the iframe capability sandbox) so it holds for every app, not app-by-app. Revoke anytime; the legal text is referenced by URL, never restated

docs.title: World
docs.summary: The spatial self-authoring shell a content-addressed desktop where every window, component and world is a self-verifying UOR object
docs.body: World is the spatial, self-authoring shell for Hologram OS Playground's malleable-object paradigm rebuilt natively on the UOR substrate. The desktop is an infinite canvas of draggable windows; each window mounts a holospace through the single-link launcher (A28) as an isolated, capability-bounded iframe with every byte re-derived to its κ (Law L5). The scene graph is a content-addressed CvRDT, so the whole desktop has a live, re-derivable identity that converges across peers with no server. Author a component in place and it becomes a live custom element AND a content address (holo://κ) editing it derives a new κ linked by prov:wasDerivedFrom, so self-improvement is a verifiable chain, not a mutation. Native Custom Elements + Shadow DOM zero dependencies, no framework, no CDN (Law L4). Windows are isolated holospaces mounted by one did:holo link (A28, default-deny sandbox). The scene graph is a sealed-delta CvRDT multiplayer desktop, serverless. Author a component → a live block AND a κ; edits are a prov:wasDerivedFrom chain (A27). Open a holospace from a holospace, without bound verifyDeep at every level

docs.body: Bare metal boot → hologram in the hologram native browser Status: scaffolded . The builder is (mkosi + cage, in ); the QEMU boot proof is (spec id , asserts after a real OS boot). Build it with . Pills go green only when QEMU witnesses no false green. Vision Hologram is the OS; the browser is its shell. A device powers on and boots straight into the hologram native browser (CEF/Chromium 138), fullscreen, showing hologram no desktop, no login. This is the ChromeOS pattern, content addressed. Architecture (the kiosk OS image) Base: a minimal rootfs (Alpine ~5 MB, or Debian via debootstrap). Display: (wlroots single app kiosk compositor) on DRM/KMS the standard "boot to one fullscreen app" path. Needs a GPU/virtio gpu with KMS. Engine: bundle + + CEF resources (use the minimal CEF dist, ~250 MB, not the 1.4 GB debug standard dist) + the handler. Init: a one line service . Content: the holospaces app served locally (a tiny static server in the image) or from the κ store; loads verified by re derivation. Output: a raw disk image → boot in QEMU ( ), the bare metal stand in; it to USB for real hardware. Witness (how it earns "Verified") stays Not implemented / Unverified until: the image boots in QEMU to the hologram native browser and CDP (over a forwarded ) confirms i.e. hologram booted inside the booted OS's browser. That reuses the Phase 3 e2e witness, one layer down. Differential vs a stock Chromium kiosk image proves it's our engine. Honest caveats (why "any device" is the hard word) Per architecture images. One x86 64 image boots most PCs (UEFI). ARM (phones, Raspberry Pi, Apple Silicon) needs a separate kernel + rootfs + CEF for ARM build. There is no single "any device" image. Mobile (Android/iOS) is not a bootable image target at all Android needs CEF for Android (a different embedding); iOS forbids non WebKit engines. "Boot on any device" excludes phones in the bare metal sense. Graphics drivers are the usual bare metal pain virtio gpu works in QEMU; real hardware needs the right KMS driver in the kernel. Size. This is a Chromium image hundreds of MB minimum. Not "light". Steps to build it (the real work, when ready) 1. / /Alpine: assemble a minimal rootfs. 2. Add + seatd + the minimal CEF dist + the holospaces app. 3. Add the init service ( ). 4. Kernel + initramfs + bootloader → a bootable raw image. 5. Boot in QEMU ( ); witness over CDP. Then to USB for real metal.

docs.title: ADR Chromium (CEF) as the κ-addressed browser holospace
docs.summary: ADR Chromium (CEF) as the κ addressed browser holospace Status: Proposed (scaffold only; not implemented). Promote into chapter 09 (Architecture Decisions) via the doc toolchain
docs.body: ADR Chromium (CEF) as the κ addressed browser holospace Status: Proposed (scaffold only; not implemented). Promote into chapter 09 (Architecture Decisions) via the doc toolchain before build. Context holospaces already runs inside Chrome today (the wasm peer, vscode web, WebGPU, SharedArrayBuffer). The goal is a hologram native browser that is Chrome equivalent, runs every Chrome extension, content addresses what it loads, and passes the ultimate test: run hologram inside it . Decision Base the browser on Chromium via CEF (Chrome runtime) not a from scratch engine because only Chromium's real extension subsystem runs Chrome extensions (MV3), and only Chromium reliably runs hologram's existing web stack. Apply the Thorium optimization profile ( O3 / AVX2 / PGO+thinLTO, ~8–38% faster) at build time. Integrate from Rust via cef rs . Content addressing seam: route every resource load through CEF's / (the Network Service's custom underneath) into the , verified by re derivation (Law L5), plus a native / scheme. Re point the HTTP cache / IndexedDB / extension storage at the content addressed store so the browser's state is κ addressed. Consequences Non goal: lightweight. Chrome equivalence + all extensions is Chromium (~30 GB source, ~150–250 MB binary, GB scale RAM). This is recorded as an explicit non goal so the trade off is not rediscovered later. "Light" here means one shared κ addressed runtime, deduped and streamed not a small engine. 100% content addressable applies to content loads at first; covering every I/O subsystem (cookies, GPU shader cache, fonts, telemetry) is a phased follow on. Extensions are MV3 only (Google sunsetting MV2). Conformance (witnesses none pass until the build lands) External authorities, pinned, witnessed against never self reference: Spec row Authority Witness the pinned CEF 138.0.62+g6981a09+chromium 138.0.7204.310 client distribution (138 LTS branch, ~8 mo extra security fixes); verified against CEF's published SHA1 on fetch boots, reports its Chromium version; commit the binary's sha256 here once extracted Chrome DevTools Protocol a headless instance answers the Chrome extension host (MV3) + the κ serving path unwritten load a known MV3 extension, assert it activates, and a resource served from the κ store re derives. Until written, the row stays Unverified . All three are Unverified today. They turn Verified only when the CI job runs the pinned CEF and the witness passes the dashboard pill is derived from , never hand set. Alternatives rejected Servo / Verso (Rust native) memory safe and embeddable, but runs zero Chrome extensions and not all of hologram's WebGPU/SAB stack. Fails the hard requirement. Reconsider only if the extension requirement is dropped. Electron / Tauri system WebView or per app Chromium; no real extension store. Rejected.

docs.title: Implementation plan the κ-addressed browser holospace (Chromium / CEF)
docs.summary: Implementation plan the κ addressed browser holospace (Chromium / CEF) Status: proposed . Companion to CHROMIUM BROWSER ADR.md. Promote both into ch.09 via the doc toolchain befo
docs.body: Implementation plan the κ addressed browser holospace (Chromium / CEF) Status: proposed . Companion to CHROMIUM BROWSER ADR.md. Promote both into ch.09 via the doc toolchain before work starts. 0 · Honest scope (read first) Goal. A hologram native browser that is Chrome equivalent, runs hologram inside it (the ultimate test), content addresses everything it loads, and runs MV3 Chrome extensions. In scope: Chromium via CEF (Chrome runtime); the loading seam; a / scheme; MV3 extension host; the browser packaged as a κ addressed holospace; Thorium build profile. Explicit non goals (do not promise these): Lightweight. Chrome equivalence is Chromium (~30 GB source, ~150–250 MB binary, GB scale RAM). "Light" only means: one shared κ runtime, deduped + streamed. Literal 100% content addressing. Content loads are addressed in Phase 2; every byte of browser state (cookies, GPU shader cache, fonts, telemetry) is a phased follow on (Phase 5), a milestone, not a switch. All extensions including MV2. MV2 is sunset by Google. "All" means all MV3 , and CEF extension fidelity is very good not total (some /enterprise surfaces). Total parity ⇒ a full Chromium browser build, still MV3 bounded. Every claim below earns a green pill only when its witness passes ( + ); nothing is hand set. 1 · Architecture Engine: CEF, Chrome runtime (Alloy removed ~M127), pinned 138 LTS . Embedder: (tauri apps fork) Rust, alongside the substrate. Loading seam: + (the Network Service underneath) → resolve URL→κ, fetch from , re derive before the renderer sees it. Build: Thorium optimization profile ( O3 / AVX2 / PGO+thinLTO) for perf. 2 · Phases (each gates a Specs row) Phase Deliverable Witness (executable) Turns Verified 0 · Pin & provenance (scaffolded) CI fetches CEF 138 LTS client dist, verifies vs CEF's published SHA1; commit the exact build + sha256 (provenance) + CI fetch step (provenance half of ) 1 · Embed & boot minimal shell boots Chromium headless; answers CDP + CDP , 2 · Content addressed loads /scheme → ; registered standard+secure; verify by re derivation; a forged byte is refused new serve a page+asset by κ; assert it re derives and a tampered byte is rejected (L5) new row 3 · Run hologram inside it (the ultimate test) Load the holospaces app, vscode web, a WebGPU + SharedArrayBuffer (COOP/COEP) page in the native browser new (CDP driven) assert the wasm peer boots + a κ served re derives new row 4 · MV3 extensions Extension host on; Open VSX gallery wired; install + activate a real MV3 extension new (CDP) load known MV3 ext, assert it activates (status bar/command), mirrors CC 19 5 · Content address the rest Re point HTTP cache → IndexedDB → cookies at , one subsystem at a time extend the kappa witness per subsystem (each is its own green) tighten toward "as close to 100% as feasible" 6 · Perf + package Apply Thorium profile; ship the browser binary itself κ addressed (verify on load), deduped + streamed reproducible build witness (binary κ stable) + a Speedometer 3 delta vs stock perf is reported, not a pass/fail row 3 · Conformance matrix (what proves "done") ← Phase 1 boots + CDP, provenance verified (Phase 0). ← Phase 1 CDP handshake. (new) ← Phase 2 re derivation + tamper refusal; tightened in Phase 5. (new) ← Phase 3 e2e (the ultimate test). ← Phase 4 extension activation. Add the three new rows to as the phases land (authority defined now, witness added with the component the repo's discipline). runs each witness in CI and writes ; the dashboard pills are derived from it. 4 · Effort & sequencing (estimates, not commitments) Rough engineer time, assuming a senior C++/Rust + Chromium savvy build: Phase 0: ~days (mostly done finalize the committed pin). Phase 1: ~1–2 weeks (cef rs shell + CDP; CEF build/toolchain setup is the cost). Phase 2: ~3–6 weeks (the loading seam is the heart; Network Service interception + κ resolution + scheme registration + re derivation). Phase 3: ~1–2 weeks (mostly integration + COOP/COEP/WebGPU verification). Phase 4: ~2–4 weeks (extension host wiring + Open VSX + the MV3 fidelity caveats). Phase 5: ~open ended (per subsystem; prioritize cache→IndexedDB→cookies). Phase 6: ~1–2 weeks for the Thorium profile + κ packaging; perf tuning ongoing. Critical path: Phase 2 (content addressing seam). Phases 4 and 5 can run in parallel once Phase 2 lands. A maintenance treadmill (tracking Chromium 138 LTS security updates, re pinning, re witnessing) is permanent, not a one off. 5 · Risks (ranked) 1. Chromium maintenance treadmill tracking upstream + re deriving pins forever. Mitigate: pin LTS (138), automate the fetch+verify+witness in CI. 2. Loading seam depth (Phase 2) the Network Service has many request paths (service workers, range requests, prefetch, CORS). Getting all loads through the κ store without breaking the web is the hard part. Mitigate: start with the main path, witness drive coverage, expand. 3. CEF extension fidelity not 100% of desktop Chrome's surface. Mitigate: witness against the target extension set; escalate to a full Chromium build only if a required API is missing. 4. Headless/CDP specifics in cefsimple/cefclient Phase 1's first CI run may need flag iteration ( , fallback to ). Low risk: witness skips, never false passes. 5. Sandbox + security re pointing storage and intercepting loads must not weaken Chromium's sandbox or the re derivation guarantee. Treat as a security review gate before Phase 5 ships. 6. Build cost / CI minutes Chromium scale. Mitigate: client distribution (prebuilt) for witnesses; full custom build only for releases. 6 · Definition of done (per the honest scope) A claim is "done" only when its row is Verified by a passing witness: , , , , . "100%" and "all" are deliberately absent from the definition of done see §0.

docs.title: Holo Cloud the hologram-native personal cloud
docs.summary: Holo Cloud the hologram native personal cloud is Nextcloud, hologram native : a completely serverless , end to end encrypted , content addressed personal cloud running in any bro
docs.body: Holo Cloud the hologram native personal cloud is Nextcloud, hologram native : a completely serverless , end to end encrypted , content addressed personal cloud running in any browser tab desktop or phone. It is the honest answer to "install and run Nextcloud": Nextcloud's storage model and protocols (WebDAV + the OCS Share API) and its Hub shape (Files at the centre, the apps one click away), with no PHP server, no database, no account files are chunked into a deduplicated κ store, persist in the browser (OPFS), and a shared file IS its content address ( ), verified against its κ before it is used (Law L5). How it works Files are content, not location (Laws L1–L5). Every file is chunked into fixed 256 KiB blocks , each named by (the substrate σ axis, native in the browser via WebCrypto the same labels / mint). Each unique block is stored once, so identical files (and shared blocks across files) deduplicate (Laws L2/L3). A file becomes a manifest = ordered list of block κ plus the whole file content address . Reading re derives every block and the whole file against its κ (verify by re derivation, L5): a peer can withhold but never forge a byte. The store is the memory (L3), in the browser. Blocks live in OPFS (the Origin Private File System) under , and the directory tree in so your cloud survives reloads with no server . When OPFS is unavailable (e.g. the Node witness) the same store falls back to RAM. Sharing & multiuser are serverless and E2E. Share any folder and Holo Cloud opens a session on the repo's content blind κ pub/sub (the same engine Holo Docs and Holo Meet use). The directory tree is a CvRDT (an LWW map of path→node), so concurrent edits converge with no coordinator. A peer downloads a file it never had by fetching its blocks sealed (AES 256 GCM, room key HKDF derived from the link secret) and announced by κ then unsealing and re verifying each block (L5). The relay only ever sees ciphertext on an unguessable topic : content blind. The Nextcloud SPEC, spoken client side (no server). implements Nextcloud's storage protocols WebDAV (RFC 4918: a with / / props; · · · · · · ) and the OCS Share API ( : a public link, shareType 3) over the native κ store. The DAV listing even carries each file's SHA256 (the κ). This mirrors : it also speaks the real protocol against a genuine Nextcloud server ( + , Basic app password auth), so Holo Cloud doubles as a real Nextcloud client the native source is content addressed (a κ that re derives), the remote one location addressed (a server URL), honestly labelled . The Hub. A Nextcloud style left app rail and Dashboard tie the suite together: the content addressed Storage meter (logical vs on disk bytes, dedup saving), Recent files, and tiles that launch the sibling holospaces Docs ( ), Talk ( ), Music ( ) handing each the same room so a meeting, a co edited document, and your files share one space. Photos is a content addressed image gallery with a lightbox; audio/video files open in their players. Lean. No CDN, no vendored framework, no CRDT library the engine is the platform's own WebCrypto + OPFS + the repo's κ pub/sub. Two small modules ( + ) and one page. Use it Open . It seeds a starter cloud (Documents · Photos · Music · Talk + a Readme), self verifies its own κ (the and in the chrome), and you're in. Drag files onto the window (or press Upload ) to add them; click a file to open it, the ⤓ to download (the bytes are re derived against their κ a mismatch is refused), ✎ to rename, 🗑 to delete. Press ⤴ Share this folder (or the chrome Share ) to mint a public link the room secret rides the URL and is never sent to any server ; anyone who opens it joins live, sees presence avatars, and their changes converge in real time. The left rail switches to Photos, Talk, Docs, Music, and Activity. Interop: / opens a folder; joins a shared space; uses a specific relay (default: a public MQTT broker). Nestable: a parent holospace hands us a room over ; we answer . Serve with cross origin isolation: → open . docs.body: DOS holospace imported component provenance The DOS holospace ( , ) boots a real x86 PC entirely from imported open source components. This file is the authoritative record of what is vendored, from where , at which version , and how it is verified the same discipline as for the Linux path. Each component is pinned by content hash (the sha256 is its κ identity); where the upstream ships a literal version string it is recorded too. A row is "verified" only once a conformance witness exists (see Conformance gap below) until then it is Pinned , not Verified. Component Authority / source Version Vendored at sha256 v86 (x86 PC emulator) copy.sh https://github.com/copy/v86 no upstream release; pinned by hash v86 core (Wasm) copy.sh https://github.com/copy/v86 no upstream release; pinned by hash SeaBIOS (x86 firmware) SeaBIOS project https://www.seabios.org/ (literal in binary) VGABIOS (video firmware) bundled with v86 https://github.com/copy/v86 no banner; pinned by hash FreeDOS FreeDOS Project https://www.freedos.org/ kernel (literal in floppy) Canonical toolchain (produces the disk no hand written FAT code) Tool Authority / source Version Used by mtools (mpartition / mformat / mcopy) GNU mtools https://www.gnu.org/software/mtools/ the reference user space FAT implementation pinned in CI ( ; record the installed version in the CI log) Specs implemented (by the canonical tool, not by hand) FAT16 + MBR on disk format Microsoft FAT: General Overview of On Disk Format , v1.03 ( ). The image is written by mtools the reference FAT implementation in : writes the MBR partition table, writes the FAT16 BPB/FATs, copies the tree in. The bytes therefore conform to the spec by construction ; that file holds only deterministic glue (fixed volume serial + source mtimes ⇒ byte identical output; the image hash is its κ). To change the on disk format, bump the pinned mtools version never hand edit FAT bytes. Intel 64 & IA 32 instruction set the ISA v86 emulates; authority is the Intel SDM (325462). Verifying the pins Conformance status FAT16 / MBR now produced by the canonical mtools toolchain (conformant by construction). confirms it end to end: an independent FAT spec reader recovers every file byte identically (round trip) and two builds are byte identical (stable κ). once mtools is present (CI) → the Specs row turns Verified . Without mtools the witness skips ( ) and the row stays Unverified no simulation. DOS boot v86 vs QEMU boots the same pinned FreeDOS floppy on v86 (the SUT) and (the reference x86 machine) and asserts both emit the same marker on the serial line the marker produced by FreeDOS's own (no authored guest binary). On pass it witnesses the whole boot chain: v86 / IA 32 / SeaBIOS / FreeDOS . It runs in the CI job (mtools + installed); absent those tools it skips ( ) and those rows stay Unverified no simulation. VGABIOS stays Unverified : the serial boot differential loads it but does not check video output. Witnessing it needs a screen capture differential (v86 framebuffer vs QEMU dump) not yet written. Regenerate the committed disk must be rebuilt with the canonical builder so the shipped disk is the mtools output, not the old hand written writer's:

docs.title: Holo EVM the Ethereum Virtual Machine, hologram-native
docs.summary: Holo EVM the Ethereum Virtual Machine, hologram native Holo EVM is a real, spec faithful Ethereum Virtual Machine realized as a holospace: execute genuine EVM bytecode and signed
docs.body: Holo EVM the Ethereum Virtual Machine, hologram native Holo EVM is a real, spec faithful Ethereum Virtual Machine realized as a holospace: execute genuine EVM bytecode and signed transactions, deploy and call contracts, watch the full opcode level trace with exact gas entirely in the browser , with all state content addressed and κ verified. No node, no server, no RPC required to run the machine. What it is A single self contained page ( ) over the vendored EthereumJS reference engine ( ) driven by a module Web Worker ( ) so the UI stays live. It reads like every sibling holospace: a thin chrome bar (identity · · Law L5 · peers), the self manage affordance, the theme, the teleport share, the mobile conformance layer, and a nestable postMessage protocol. Faithful to the source (the EVM spec) The engine is the EthereumJS reference implementation the Ethereum Foundation's JavaScript EVM/VM that tracks the Yellow Paper + the executable spec and passes the official consensus suite. Holo EVM vendors it unmodified ( v10.1.2 + ), bundled to one browser ES module no CDN so the full opcode set, gas schedule, precompiles and active hardfork rules (Cancun) are the spec's, not a lookalike. Reproduce the bundle with . UOR: Ethereum is already content addressed Holo EVM leans into the fact that Ethereum's state model is a UOR content addressed system, so the mapping is exact (κ axis = ): State is a κ store. Account/storage live in a Merkle Patricia trie whose every node is keyed by its keccak256 hash. The trie is backed by a the page persists as a JSON snapshot of ; on reload it rebuilds from the snapshot + the saved root and resumes byte for byte ("the store is the memory"). The state root is a κ label surfaced in the chrome bar as and stamped into the teleport share link. Code is addressed by codeHash = keccak256(code) content addressing of contract code. What you can do Run execute raw bytecode statelessly; see return data, gas, and a full opcode trace (pc · op · per step gas · stack), plus final stack, memory, storage writes and logs. Deploy a real signed creation transaction; the contract's code + state persist into the κ store and the state root advances. Call a real signed message call to any deployed address. Live network connect to Ethereum mainnet over public JSON RPC: latest blocks, base fee, gas, and the latest block's transactions. Every block's 16 field header is RLP re encoded and keccak hashed in browser ( ) and κ checked against the network's block hash the badge (Law L5), no simulation. κ Fork a trustless mainnet fork (the thing that was impossible) A normal fork (Hardhat/Anvil) trusts the RPC : it believes whatever state the node hands back. Holo EVM's κ Fork does not. When you call a real deployed contract, it: 1. re derives the block header from raw consensus bytes (RLP→keccak) → the κ; 2. forks over a public RPC and executes the call with the real EVM, recording every account whose code/balance is touched and every storage slot read (from the trace, including CALL/DELEGATECALL/EXTCODE\ targets so proxies are covered); 3. pulls an Merkle proof for each and re derives that it hashes up to that state root , and checks for every executed account. If the RPC lies by one byte, a proof fails to root and the call is flagged. The result is a light client grade, content addressed fork of mainnet in a browser tab call any contract, replay history at any block, or run counterfactual "what if" calls, all over cryptographically verified state, trusting no server . Witnessed live: WETH, USDC (proxy → implementation, both code κ verified). The verification primitive is witnessed offline against a pinned fixture ( ). κ Witness a portable, self verifying slice of Ethereum (The Verge, in a tab) This is the part that is genuinely impossible without a content addressed substrate. A κ witness is a tiny, content addressed object the re derived block header + exactly the Merkle proofs a computation needs + the verified code whose κ is of its canonical (sorted key) bytes. Mint one for any mainnet read; then anyone can verify it fully offline : 1. the network is physically disabled (the worker overrides to throw); 2. the block header is re derived from raw bytes (RLP→keccak) → must equal the witness's block hash; 3. every Merkle proof is re rooted to that header's , and for each account; 4. a partial state is rebuilt from only the witness and the real EVM re executes the call, reproducing the exact result. If a single byte is flipped, the κ changes and the proofs stop rooting the witness is refused. This is statelessness (The Verge): the verifier holds no chain, just the witness, and re derives everything; and it's only possible because Ethereum state is keccak content addressed, exactly like UOR. An explorer hands you data you must trust; a κ witness hands you a proof your own device (a phone, offline) re derives. The κ Witness tab does both halves on one screen mint here, verify offline and the witness is shareable (download / copy / teleport by its κ). Witnessed live (USDC proxy → implementation, both code κ verified; tampered proof refused) and offline against a pinned witness ( ): header re derives, proofs root, the EVM re executes to reproduce the result, all with zero network. Full block verifier re execute a whole block, reproduce the post state root (The Verge) Where κ Witness verifies a single call , the full block verifier verifies an entire block's state transition the exact check a validating node performs. Load a self contained Ethereum Foundation consensus vector ( BlockchainTests: complete pre state alloc + the RLP block(s) + the expected post state root), and Holo EVM: 1. loads the pre state into a Merkle Patricia κ store and confirms it reproduces the genesis state root ; 2. decodes the block from its RLP and re executes every transaction in the real EVM ( ); 3. recomputes the post state root and checks it equals the one committed in the block header, and that the account balances/nonces match. This is deterministic and fully offline the vector is self contained, so reproduction is pure execution + Merkle math (no node, no network). It means the same engine that runs your contracts here passes the Ethereum consensus suite, in a browser tab . The vectors are vendored and κ pinned ( , with ); the page re derives each vector's sha256 before use (Law L5) and you can paste any BlockchainTest to run it yourself. Witnessed offline: all vendored vectors re execute and reproduce their post state roots. Honest scope: this is the deterministic full block tier. Verifying an arbitrary live mainnet block the same way needs a producer supplied witness Glamsterdam's block level access lists (H2 2026) or an archive because public RPCs prune state proofs and don't ship block witnesses yet. Holo EVM's foundation (pre state witnesses that root to S₍ₙ₋₁₎, full execution) is already validated for that the moment those land. Combining with Holo Scan Holo EVM accepts a fork handoff two ways, so it composes with Holo Scan (the Etherscan identical explorer): a deep link, and a . On any mainnet contract page in Holo Scan, ⟠ Simulate · κ fork opens that contract here and runs it trustlessly explore on Holo Scan, then execute on proven state in Holo EVM. Law L5 verify by re derivation re derives the sha256 of the engine bytes it loads against the pin in and refuses a drift. In engine, it reproduces and a canonical ADD opcode trace at the exact spec gas before it enables execution. The witness ( ) re checks every pin offline and re runs the functional KATs including re deriving real mainnet block 1,000,000 byte for byte. A forged byte fails. Files the holospace page (live network view + the EVM machine) the persistent VM over the content addressed κ store the vendored EthereumJS reference bundle + + the offline Law L5 + functional witness reproducible engine (re)builder registered in ( ) and ( )

docs.body: Holo Git the hologram native git forge holospace is Holo Git : a completely serverless, end to end encrypted, multiuser git forge that runs in the tab. It is the honest answer to the Gitea brief Gitea's data model, REST API and forge feature set (repositories, code browsing, diffs, commits, branches/tags, issues, pull requests with review + 3 way merge, releases, stars, search), with none of its servers (no Go service, no database, no reverse proxy). The key idea: a git repository IS a κ store Git is already content addressed object storage. Every object blob, tree, commit, tag is named by , i.e. its oid is its content. That is exactly a UOR κ label ( = , AGENTS.md). So Holo Git does not emulate git on top of some other store it realises git's object model as the substrate's κ store, and the five laws fall out for free: 1. Content, not location an object is addressed by its oid, never a path/URL. 2. Canonical forms only we hold oids (κ) and (de)serialise git's canonical object encoding at the boundary. 3. The store is the memory (OPFS backed) is the address space; RAM caches. 4. Everything through the substrate clone/fetch/push are + over the repo's content blind κ pub/sub. A git transfer is just "fetch the reachable oids I lack" git's want/have, made trivial by content addressing (dedup exact). 5. Verify by re derivation every received object's oid is re derived and a mismatch is refused ; a lying relay can withhold but never forge. How it works Code = real git objects in the κ store. is a dependency free git core: it writes git's canonical blob/tree/commit/tag encoding, so the object ids match real byte for byte in both object formats ( , git's default for max interop; , git's modern mode + the substrate's σ axis). It does log, unified diff (Myers), merge base, and a 3 way (diff3) merge no vendored git library. The forge = a convergent CvRDT. Repositories, issues, pull requests, reviews, comments, labels and releases are a conflict free replicated data type (Lamport LWW map + causal tree RGA) over . Many people edit the same forge live, with no coordinator and no lost data refs (branch/tag pointers) included. The Gitea API, two backends, one shape. exposes one client surface with interchangeable backends (the idiom of / ): is the serverless in browser forge above; is a faithful client of the real Gitea REST API v1 ( , , , , , , , …) so the same UI also drives a live Gitea, proving the native backend conforms to Gitea's actual contract. Content blind, even for source code. Git objects ride the κ pub/sub AES 256 GCM sealed with a key HKDF derived (WebCrypto) from the room secret; the announced κ is the object's oid (a hash reveals only equality), the bytes on the wire are ciphertext. The relay sees only ciphertext on an unguessable topic. Local OPFS copies stay plaintext (your own browser). The invite is the workspace. The room secret lives in the URL fragment ( ) never sent to any server. Open the link → your browser becomes a peer in the same forge. Transport is ( ) or (public MQTT broker) by default, with an offline fallback (single user, still content addressed). Nestable + mobile. It accepts a workspace over ( ) and announces , and it adopts + a viewport. Witnesses (V&V by external ground truth never self reference) uses real as the oracle: blob, nested tree and commit oids re derive byte for byte to / / in both formats; the empty blob / empty tree / KAT constants match; Law L5 refuses a forged byte; the full forge runs in engine (commit · branch · PR · unified diff · 3 way merge · serverless clone by κ); and the Gitea REST surface is covered. Covers , , . the live functional proof under Playwright: two isolated Chromium peers in one workspace over a content blind κ relay. A repo + commit created on A appear on B, which re fetches the code objects by κ and re derives them; an issue converges; a pull request opened on A is merged by B (cross peer 3 way merge) and A sees it merged; and the relay holds only ciphertext (no file content / issue title in the clear). Witnessed 15/15. Gitea feature conformance (what's covered) The forge loop is implemented to Gitea's data model + UX, witnessed against real (node 63/63) and live in two browsers (2 peer 22/22): Repositories create (sha1/sha256, auto init), import from live Gitea, watch · star · fork (+counts), clone dropdown (holo:// + .git), topics, default branch, visibility, delete; recent activity feed on the dashboard. Code file tree, file view with generic syntax highlight, README + any rendered as GFM (task lists, tables, issue refs, , autolinks, emoji, strikethrough), language bar (linguist style), anchorable line numbers ( permalinks + range highlight), Raw , per file History , Go to file fuzzy finder, inline editor that makes real commits. Commits / Branches / Tags commit list + commit diff; dedicated Branches (new/delete, open PR) and Tags (annotated) views. Issues open/close, comments, labels (manage/apply/filter), assignees (self assign), reactions (👍👎😄🎉😕❤️🚀👀), edit title/body, sort (newest/oldest/ most commented/recently updated), cross references, milestones (basic). Pull requests create (compare branches), Conversation / Commits / Files tabs, unified diff, reviews (Approve / Request changes / Comment) with a decision summary, merge methods (merge commit / squash / rebase), draft/WIP detection and merge gate, mergeability/conflict pre flight, delete branch on merge. Releases list/create (draft/prerelease), creates a real tag. Cross cutting live multiuser presence, content blind sealed κ transport (E2E even for code), nestable (postMessage), mobile, Law L5 self κ, share by fragment. Deferred (large peripheral subsystems, not part of the core forge loop): organizations & teams, user profiles/followers, webhooks, deploy keys, branch protection, projects (kanban), notifications, 2FA / SSH GPG keys, full code search, LFS, and migrations. Run it cd os node holo serve.mjs 8200 static + COI headers, LAN bound open http://localhost:8200/git.html → "＋ New" → create a repo → commit, open an issue, branch, open a PR, merge. docs.title: Holo Docs the hologram-native office holospace
docs.summary: Holo Docs the hologram native office holospace is Holo Docs : a completely serverless, end to end encrypted, content addressed office suite Writer (Word), Calc (Excel), and Imp
docs.body: Holo Docs the hologram native office holospace is Holo Docs : a completely serverless, end to end encrypted, content addressed office suite Writer (Word), Calc (Excel), and Impress (PowerPoint) with Google Docs style real time multi user collaboration , running entirely in the tab. It is the honest answer to the LibreOffice collaboration brief (help.libreoffice.org/.../collab.html): its feature set and standards (simultaneous multi author editing, per cell conflict resolution, shared mode, User Data identity, change tracking, a single writer file lock) with none of its servers (no shared file lock host, no document service). How it works A document is a CvRDT. Every edit is a tiny conflict free replicated data type delta ; merge is the only integration primitive and it is commutative, associative and idempotent, so any set of concurrent edits converges to the same document on every peer with no coordinator and no lost data ( ): a causal tree RGA sequence (stable element ids + tombstones, siblings ordered by id) for Writer text, Impress slides, and a slide's elements convergent and interleaving free , and a Lamport timestamped LWW map for Calc cells, inline text marks, element geometry and all properties concurrent writes resolve last writer wins and the loser is retained so the LibreOffice Resolve Conflicts choice is real. Everything rides the content blind κ pub/sub. Each delta / presence / snapshot is JSON AES 256 GCM sealed with a room key HKDF derived (WebCrypto) from the URL fragment secret ( ), published as a content addressed κ object, and re derived to its κ on receipt (Law L5). The relay only ever sees ciphertext on an unguessable topic. Transport is (local relay) or (public MQTT broker) Hologram Meet's data plane, minus the media. A saved document IS its content address. hashes the document's canonical (sorted, deterministic) state → a stable, hologram native URL that re derives on every peer (document level Law L5). The invite is the app. The room secret lives in the URL fragment ( ) never sent to any server. Late joiners catch up by fetching a peer's sealed snapshot by κ and merging it serverless, content addressed state transfer. Anchored in UOR (content addressable storage · compute · networking) Storage: a document's identity is its content hash ( ); embedded images are sealed → addressed by κ → fetched by κ from whichever peer holds them (P2P content distribution, dedup + integrity, no file server); identical documents share one address. Local persistence is keyed by κ. Compute: the CRDT merge and the spreadsheet formula engine run locally in the tab (no server round trip) the source of the low latency. Formulas recalc on a dependency graph with cycle detection. Networking: every object is sealed + content addressed + verified by re derivation (Law L5) on a content blind relay a lying relay can withhold but never forge, and never sees plaintext. Lean · low latency · responsive Coalesced deltas: rapid local edits (every keystroke) are applied instantly to the local doc (responsive) but the seal+announce is coalesced e.g. 12 keystrokes collapse into one sealed κ object on the wire (fewer objects, less crypto, less network). Witnessed. Bounded memory: the κ dedup set is capped no unbounded growth in long sessions. Lean: no vendored CRDT library, no CDN, no service worker the engine is the platform's own WebCrypto + the repo's κ pub/sub; the formula engine is pure and hand rolled. Rendering is coalesced. Calc: a grid bound to a per cell LWW map; a real recursive descent formula engine ( ) A1 refs, ranges, operators, a function library (SUM, AVERAGE, IF, AND/OR/NOT, ROUND, MOD, POWER, CONCAT, LEN/LEFT/RIGHT/MID, SUMIF/COUNTIF/AVERAGEIF, VLOOKUP, …), dependency recalculation, cycle detection ( ) and error values; cell + number formatting; multiple sheets; AutoSum; export CSV / TSV. Impress: slides + text boxes, shapes, and content addressed images ; drag / resize; per element independent registers (position, style, text merge concurrently); slide rail, duplicate / delete, speaker notes; Present mode; export HTML slideshow / outline. Interoperability with the existing standards (in browser, no server): import and export the two ISO office formats OOXML (ISO/IEC 29500): .docx , .xlsx , .pptx , and ODF (ISO/IEC 26300): .odt , .ods , .odp . Spreadsheet formulas carry cached values and translate to/from the ODF dialect; Impress images embed/round trip. Exports + the sealed snapshot (the content addressed source of truth). Use it Open → choose Document / Spreadsheet / Presentation and a name. The address bar becomes the invite; anyone who opens it co edits live. Save mints the document's . Covers , , in . Functional (local): two isolated Chromium peers prove a live co edit ( 18/18 ): presence (count = 2), Writer text converges both ways to the same content address , a Calc formula recalculates on the other peer ( ), concurrent same cell edits converge with the loser retained , an Impress element adds and moves across peers, and the relay held only ciphertext . docs.body: Holo Hub the hologram native software center holospace is KDE Discover, hologram native : a graphical software center that runs entirely in the browser desktop or phone with no app store server, no package daemon, no install . It is the honest answer to "install and run KDE Discover": Discover's actual job and interface browse a catalog, read each app's metadata and reviews, install it, and launch it made serverless and content addressed over UOR , where every app is a holospace and "install" re derives the app's content address before it runs (Law L5). The mapping to Discover is exact: KDE Discover Holo Hub reads AppStream catalog metadata from the distro reads genuine AppStream 0.16 collection metadata ( ) Home: a Featured carousel + category tiles a featured hero + the three shelves Learn · Work · Play browses freedesktop categories the same freedesktop Menu spec categories, grouped into the three shelves app page: screenshots · description · install · version · license · links · what's new the same app detail page , faithfully reviews & ratings via ODRS ODRS shaped reviews (rating 0–100 = stars×20, karma) as a content addressed CvRDT Updates (newer package version) content addressed updates (installed κ ≠ current pinned κ) installs via PackageKit / Flatpak / Snap installs by re deriving the loader's κ (Law L5) and pins it; a mismatch is refused Sources / Settings the κ source (origin · catalog κ · pinned loaders · relay) The three sections The home screen shows Learn , Work and Play , derived from each component's freedesktop categories by a single, witnessed rule ( / → Learn · / / → Work · / / / / → Play · bare / → Learn ): Learn Hologram OS (Debian), QEMU, Holo Boot. The machines underneath. Work Holo Docs, Holo Cloud, Hologram Meet, Holo Git, Holo Workspace. Serverless productivity. Play Holo Video, Winamp, Holo Music, Holo Player. Content addressed media. How it works Strict adherence to AppStream. is real AppStream collection metadata , each with the required · · · (p/ul/li) · from the freedesktop Menu spec · · typed s · (the holospace loader) · · · · · / · . The Hub also ships the upstream MetaInfo form ( , a single ). ships the parser (DOMParser) the catalog is data, not hard coded. κ re derivation IS install verification (Law L5). pins, by sha256, the catalog + the Hub's own icons/screens (the owned , strict κ gate) and every installable loader page ( referenced , re pinned at build, exactly as Holo Boot does for its loaders). Before an app installs, Holo Hub fetches its bytes and re derives their κ; on a match it records ✓ κ verified , on a mismatch it refuses the install ("✗ κ MISMATCH install refused"). Launch mounts the holospace full viewport (nestable the frame architecture). ODRS reviews, serverless. Reviews follow the Open Desktop Ratings model (rating 0–100 = stars×20, summary/description/version, helpful up/down karma) but are a content addressed CvRDT : each review's id is the κ of its immutable body (so it dedupes), and reviews converge over a same origin BroadcastChannel substrate (and an optional relay) with no server . Left rail (Home · Learn · Work · Play · Installed · Updates · Sources · About), a search box with Ctrl+F focus, the featured hero, app cards with rating stars + install state, and the full app detail page (screenshots carousel + lightbox, rendered AppStream description, What's New from , license/version/developer/links, content rating, reviews with a write form + karma). No framework, no build, no CDN. is one self contained page (vanilla JS + CSS, like every shipped holospace); the engine is the platform + for κ (blake3 via the engine wasm when present, else SHA 256). Use it Open . Type to search (Ctrl+F), browse Learn/Work/Play , write a review (star + headline + body), and see the Updates view light up when an installed app's content address drifts from the catalog. On mobile it just runs the rail collapses behind ☰, the shelves scroll, and there is no horizontal overflow (WCAG 2.2 §1.4.10), via the shared , witnessed at 375×812. Interop / nesting contract: joins reviews over a relay · a parent holospace can or to deep link the store; the Hub announces to its parent. Serve it: → open . Regenerate the catalog + re pin the loaders deterministically with . Verify Pure (local + CI): Law L5 (owned assets re derive byte for byte; referenced loaders are valid κ + genuine pages; a forged loader is refused); is well formed AppStream collection metadata with every required field; every is a freedesktop Menu category; an independent oracle maps every component into exactly one of Learn/Work/Play (all three non empty) and the engine implements the same precedence; the ODRS model (stars×20, histogram, CvRDT, κ addressed ids); and 's Discover profile (parser, install with refusal, nested launch, search + Ctrl+F, Updates, Sources, mobile, nesting, teleport). Screenshots: . Honest limits Catalog = the deploy, not the world. Discover reads whatever AppStream metadata the distro ships; Holo Hub reads , the curated set of holospaces in this origin. docs.title: Hologram Meet the hologram-native conferencing holospace
docs.summary: Hologram Meet the hologram native conferencing holospace is Hologram Meet : a completely serverless, end to end encrypted video meeting that runs in the tab. It is the honest ans
docs.body: Hologram Meet the hologram native conferencing holospace is Hologram Meet : a completely serverless, end to end encrypted video meeting that runs in the tab. It is the honest answer to the Jitsi Meet brief Jitsi's feature set and standards (a tiled WebRTC call with mute/camera/screen share/chat/reactions/raise hand/recording), with none of its servers (no Prosody XMPP, no Videobridge SFU, no Jicofo). How it works Media = a peer to peer WebRTC mesh. One per pair; audio and video flow directly between browsers over DTLS SRTP genuinely end to end, no middlebox. W3C "perfect negotiation" (glare safe) in . Everything else rides the content blind κ pub/sub. Signalling, presence, reactions, chat, and shared files are each JSON (or bytes) AES 256 GCM sealed with a key HKDF derived (WebCrypto) from the room secret, published as a content addressed κ object, and re derived to its κ on receipt (Law L5). The relay only ever sees ciphertext on an unguessable topic. Transport is (local relay, ) or (public MQTT broker) by default. The invite is the app. The room secret lives in the URL fragment ( ) never sent to any server. Open the link → your browser becomes a peer. STUN, not TURN. STUN only discovers your public address (sees no media); there is deliberately no TURN relay (it would carry media, breaking serverless + private). How it leverages UOR content addressing Security / privacy / integrity: every off RTP object is sealed + addressed by κ + verified by re derivation (Law L5) a lying relay can withhold but never forge, and it stays content blind. Lean: no vendored binary, no CDN the "engine" is the platform's own WebRTC + WebCrypto. Low latency: media is direct P2P (no server hop). Scalability for shared content: files are sealed → addressed by κ → fetched by κ from whichever peer holds them and verified on receipt a P2P content distribution layer (dedup + integrity) with no file server. For live media, the engine down scales outbound resolution as the roster grows (no simulcast/SVC without an SFU). Feature set (serverless achievable Jitsi parity) Multi party audio/video · tiled gallery + spotlight/pin (auto spotlight on share) · screen share · raise hand · emoji reactions · in call chat · content addressed file sharing · participants panel (name, mic/cam, raised, connection quality) · device selection (camera/mic/speaker) · per peer connection quality indicator · picture in picture · local recording (client side itself content addressable) · in call rename · active speaker highlight · adaptive video quality + Holo Compositor (below) · keyboard shortcuts incl. push to talk · mobile + desktop responsive · nestable via . Best video quality on any device (the Holo Compositor) Two cooperating halves deliver the best available quality, up to 4K: Send side adaptive ladder. The outbound capture resolution + per peer encode bitrate are chosen from a 180p→4K ladder by device hardware ( / / mobile / display) × measured uplink ( , with the Network Information API as a hint) ÷ roster size , applied via + . It ramps up cautiously and drops on congestion immediately. 4K is reached when a capable device, a fast uplink, and a small call (≤2 peers) all allow it and a 4K screen share always requests native resolution at a high bitrate. Receive side the Holo Compositor. Each large/spotlit tile is rendered through (the repo's WebGPU present layer): zero copy + a Lanczos super resolution pass reconstructs the received stream toward the display's native pixels (e.g. a bandwidth limited 720p stream → toward 4K on a 4K screen). One GPU device per tile, so it enhances selectively (spotlight, or all tiles in a small call) and falls back to plain wherever WebGPU is absent or can't render it never shows a worse picture than the raw stream. Manual override. A quality menu (Auto · 4K · 1080p · 720p · 360p · Data saver) in the dock and in Settings pins the send tier; a ✦ HD toggle turns the compositor on/off. Per tile badges show the delivered resolution ( = reconstructed by the compositor). Server only features, intentionally excluded (they would violate the serverless constraint): cloud recording / live streaming, server side transcription / captions, SIP / PSTN dial in, server enforced moderation. Virtual backgrounds are omitted for leanness (they require a vendored ML segmentation model). Use it Open → a fresh room is minted and the address bar becomes the invite. Copy it ( invite ) and send it; anyone who opens it joins the same room. Locally: then open , served by e.g. . Verify Pure (local + CI): static + transport codec checks (real WebRTC mesh, perfect negotiation, no SFU/TURN, sealed content addressed signalling + files, the full feature set, the adaptive quality ladder + Holo Compositor, mobile, nestable). Wired into as , covering the , , rows in . Functional (local): two fake media Chromium peers prove a live call (16/16): both reach , each renders the other's decoded video tile, chat + raise hand + reactions + a content addressed file (fetched by κ, verified, E2E decrypted) all deliver, the manual quality override pins a tier and auto re enables, the device profile drives the ceiling, the Holo Compositor is present (or degrades cleanly), and the relay held only ciphertext. captures a screenshot. Honest limits Mesh scales to ~2–8 participants (each peer uploads to every other); bigger meetings need an SFU = a media server, which serverless forbids. Symmetric NAT without TURN: the direct path may fail (no media relay by design). iOS Safari: no the Share control is disabled there. Public broker mode is live only (no replay) and caps files at ~240 KB; use a local relay for larger files / determinism. Files · (the mesh engine) · · . Reuses / / (transport), (local relay), and (mobile conformance).

docs.title: Holo Messenger → Mattermost parity implementation plan
docs.summary: Holo Messenger → Mattermost parity implementation plan Goal: match Mattermost's feature set and UX/UI (information architecture + light "Denim" default and dark themes), while th
docs.body: Holo Messenger → Mattermost parity implementation plan Goal: match Mattermost's feature set and UX/UI (information architecture + light "Denim" default and dark themes), while the backend stays native to Hologram every message is a content addressed, E2E sealed κ object on the existing append only DAG. The relay only ever sees ciphertext. Decisions locked: Mattermost structure, both themes (light Denim default + dark). Backend model unchanged in spirit; we only add new sealed record types. The one load bearing constraint Content addressed means immutable . A κ object can never change. So every Mattermost action that "mutates" a message (edit, delete, pin) is instead a new append only record that references a target κ . The render layer folds these records into the final view. This is the same trick already used for reactions ( ), generalized. Authorization is intrinsic: every record is signed by its author. An edit or delete is honored only if . No server enforces this the render layer does, and signatures make it unforgeable. Sealed body schema (extended) All records are JSON inside the AEAD sealed body, discriminated by : meaning fields notes (none) message existing reaction existing edit newest edit by same author wins; show "(edited)" delete (tombstone) author only; renders "message deleted" pin channel scoped pinned set Personal state (Saved/Flagged, read position, hidden channels, theme) is per user and not shared stored in keyed by κ. Mattermost's Saved posts are per user too, so this matches semantics with zero protocol cost. Channel registry (dynamic channels) uses a dedicated meta channel whose κ/key derive from the workspace secret ( ). Creating a channel posts a record there: . Every peer subscribes to meta, learns the channel set, and derives each channel's κ/key from exactly as today. Channels stop being a hardcoded const. Status extends the existing presence payload: . Phase 1 Message operations + schema (backend touching) The table stakes gap. Extend the schema and render fold; reuse the reaction path. Edit ( ), delete ( ), pin ( ) records + author checked render fold. Save/flag (local), copy permalink ( ), deep link scroll to. Per message hover action bar + "⋯" overflow menu (react, reply, save, pin, edit, delete, copy link). "(edited)" indicator; tombstone rendering; pinned set per channel. Acceptance: edit/delete only by author; pins visible to all; saved/permalink survive reload; relay still sees only ciphertext (existing witness test extended). Phase 2 Channels, people, autocomplete (model + UI) Dynamic channels via the meta channel: create / browse / join / leave / hide. (Public = workspace room key model, as today. True private channels via the existing HPKE path are deferred to Phase 4.) Sidebar categories: Favorites / Channels / Direct messages, unread grouping, collapse, "+" to create or browse. Mattermost sidebar behavior. Status model: online/away/dnd/offline + custom status; idle auto away; status dots everywhere; profile popover on avatar/name click. Autocomplete popups in composer: members, channels, emoji. Acceptance: a channel created on one peer appears on another; status propagates; autocomplete inserts correct mention/channel/emoji. Phase 3 Mattermost skin + global surfaces (mostly UI) Theme system: the CSS is already var driven. Add a light Denim token block + dark block under , toggle in Settings, persist. Denim light is the default (indigo sidebar, white center) per Mattermost. Global header: logo/workspace, global search box, recent mentions, saved, settings, profile+status menu. Global search across all locally decrypted transcripts (in memory index). Right panel tabs: Thread / Members / Pinned / Files / Channel info. Global Threads view, Saved view, Recent Mentions view. New messages line; unread counts; jump to unread. Acceptance: light/dark toggle clean across every surface; search finds messages in any channel; right panel tabs all populate. Phase 4 Depth + the security upgrade Slash commands ( ), message drafts, message priority/acknowledgements, notification settings, emoji picker (full set + search), file grid + lightbox. Wire MLS / RFC 9420 ( ) behind the same UI for forward secrecy + post compromise security; private channels via per member HPKE key sealing. Acceptance: MLS group forms in tab; a rotated epoch key locks out a removed member; private channel undecryptable without a sealed key. What stays exactly as is κ content addressing, signed message DAG, AEAD sealed bodies ( , ). Workspace secret in fragment invite model; broker/relay transport seeing only ciphertext on unguessable topics. Zero server, static site deployment to GitHub Pages. Risks / open questions Search scope: only channels you hold keys for and have synced locally are searchable there is no server index. Acceptable for the room key model. Public broker history gap: edits/deletes/pins arriving while offline aren't replayed by a stateless broker (same limit as messages today). A durable relay fixes it; out of scope until transport is upgraded. Single team: Mattermost has team switching. We are one workspace per invite; the global header keeps the slot but multi team is deferred.

docs.title: Holo Music the hologram-native music holospace
docs.summary: Holo Music the hologram native music holospace is Navidrome, hologram native : a lean, content addressed music library and player that runs entirely in the browser desktop or p
docs.body: Holo Music the hologram native music holospace is Navidrome, hologram native : a lean, content addressed music library and player that runs entirely in the browser desktop or phone with no Go server, no database, no install . It is the honest answer to "install and run Navidrome": Navidrome's actual external contract the Subsonic API / OpenSubsonic (the protocol Navidrome implements) and its react admin music library UX (browse Albums · Artists · Songs · Genres · Playlists, a persistent queue player, favorites, star ratings, play counts) made serverless and content addressed over UOR , with every byte κ verified before it plays (Law L5). How it works The κ store is the library. is the hologram native analog of Navidrome's media scanner: it walks the music tree ( ), reads each file's metadata straight from its embedded RIFF tags ( exactly as a real server reads ID3/Vorbis tags), content addresses every audio file and album cover, and emits a Subsonic shaped catalog ( : artists · albums · songs( ) · genres · playlists) plus pinning it. No DB the manifest is the catalog, identical files dedup, and every object re derives to its κ. Strict adherence to the Subsonic spec. is the data layer: one render shape, two sources. The native source serves the κ store catalog client side; the server source speaks the real Subsonic REST API with salted token auth ( · · · · · ) over the documented endpoints . So Holo Music doubles as a genuine Navidrome/Subsonic client : point it at your own server. The bundled MD5 is proven against the Subsonic spec vector ( ). Content addressed by default. The bundled library is original, royalty free music (CC0) synthesized deterministically by (a seeded PRNG over fixed musical scales no randomness, no external sample), so every track re derives byte for byte to a fixed κ on any machine. Album covers are procedural SVGs, also content addressed. 3 albums · 11 tracks · 3 genres · 2 curated playlists. κ verified playback (Law L5). On every track change the player re derives the playing bytes' sha256 and shows ✓ κ verified in the chrome (and the κ address of the track); a forged byte fails the match and playback is refused , just like Holo Video / Winamp. No framework, no build, no CDN. is one self contained page (vanilla JS + CSS, like every shipped holospace); the engine is the platform for audio, a Web Audio for the player's spectrum, the Media Session API for lock screen transport, for Law L5 verification. The whole internet as one library Universe search The top search box is a federated "Universe" search : one query fans out in parallel across your κ store, the Internet Archive , live radio, and podcasts, and each group streams into the page as its source answers so it feels instant and alive. It turns "the entire internet" into a single, discoverable, instantly playable library. Internet Archive is the on demand backbone millions of full tracks/albums (Live Music Archive concerts, netlabels, 78s), via its open Advanced Search + Metadata API (no key, CORS). Holo Music field scopes the query to music (creator/title, requires an MP3 derivative, excludes the big spoken word collections), so results read like a record shop, not an audio dump. Each item is an album you open → track list → play; tracks stream straight from . Paste any link (the limitless escape hatch): an archive.org page → its album; a direct / /… → a playable track; a / → a live stream; anything else → an honest "open to import." does the routing; paste a URL anywhere and it resolves in the search. Save anything → content addressed curation. Internet Archive albums/tracks fold into Favorites (Tracks · Albums · Stations · Channels · Podcasts), and your whole saved collection hashes to a κ you can share (Law L5). Location addressed sources are labelled honestly; the curation is content addressed. Own anything forever import to the κ store The truly limitless part: when the dev server ( ) is running, ＋ Own turns any link into a permanent, content addressed track you own offline. The server downloads + transcodes it ( ffmpeg for direct media / HLS, yt dlp vendored for YouTube / Bandcamp / SoundCloud / …) to a web decodable AAC/M4A, content addresses it (sha256 κ) , and drops it into the κ store ( ). It then appears under Imported (and in Songs / search) and plays κ verified (Law L5) like any native track re importing the same source is instant (dedup). Use it from the ＋ Own chrome button, the paste a link resolver in search, or ⬇ Own album on an Internet Archive release. Two small server capabilities make this work, both opt in (only when runs; advertises them so the UI degrades gracefully without them): runs , streaming NDJSON progress; the url is a process arg (no shell). The result re derives to its κ on play. a tiny CORS proxy for text (podcast RSS, / ), so podcast episodes load even when a feed host blocks CORS (near universal coverage). Local convenience with an SSRF guard (http(s) only, private/loopback refused, 8 MB cap). This is the honest endgame for "the entire internet": stream the open web directly, and own anything you want to keep content addressed, verifiable, yours forever. Sound quality the Holo Audio Engine is an OS wide, high quality Web Audio output engine that hologram native (κ store) tracks play through toggled with ✨ Quality in the chrome. It is honest by design (Law L5 ethos): it does not fabricate detail that was never in the bytes (no magical "upscaling"). What it really does: plays at the device native sample rate (no needless resampling); a transparent, well understood DSP graph a 5 band parametric EQ (60 · 250 · 1k · 4k · 12k Hz), a bass low shelf, an "air" high shelf (a psychoacoustic brightness shelf synthesis, labelled as such, not recovery), optional headphone crossfeed (BS2B style: a delayed, low passed cross bleed that genuinely reduces fatigue and widens the image), and a brick wall limiter so boosts never clip; a real output level meter (AnalyserNode) honest proof the signal is flowing; 8 presets (Pure · Hi Fi · Warm · Bright/Air · Bass Boost · Vocal · Headphones · Night) + a live EQ; settings persist. Pure = a bit transparent wire (+ a safety limiter). The key design constraint (and why it's clean): Web Audio's taints and silences cross origin no cors media. So Holo Music uses two audio elements plays same origin κ native tracks through the engine; plays cross origin streams (radio/SoundCloud) directly , untouched. The engine therefore applies exactly to hologram native audio and pinning a stream makes it native, unlocking enhancement . Always the highest quality at source for both streaming and import: Import stream copies a browser playable source (AAC/MP3/Opus/FLAC/ALAC/PCM) with zero generation loss so an owned κ track is the highest fidelity the source allows (hi res PCM → FLAC); yt dlp is told to prefer lossless (FLAC/ALAC) then the best sorted bitrate. Only un playable codecs are re encoded (256 k AAC). Streaming picks the best file the source offers. The Internet Archive resolver now chooses lossless FLAC per track when present (it groups each track's representations and ranks lossless first), shows the quality ( ♪ FLAC · lossless ), and streams it. If a large lossless file won't stream in browser (some IA servers don't advertise range support), it auto falls back to the high quality MP3 seamlessly while Pin still grabs the lossless original. The result: highest quality playback when possible, never a dead stream, and a lossless owned copy on demand. SoundCloud discover · stream · pin SoundCloud has no open/CORS API (registration closed for years; is CORS locked and uses signed, expiring URLs), so the browser can't talk to it directly. Holo Music bridges it honestly through the host's yt dlp (the maintained SoundCloud extractor) via 's routes: Discover a SoundCloud nav view (revealed when is true): search + trending chips (Lo Fi · House · Beats · Hip Hop · Chi

docs.title: The `OS` holospace a real Debian arm64 box
docs.summary: The holospace a real Debian arm64 box is a holospace that boots a real, unmodified Linux kernel + a Debian arm64 root filesystem on the holospaces system emulator and presents it
docs.body: The holospace a real Debian arm64 box is a holospace that boots a real, unmodified Linux kernel + a Debian arm64 root filesystem on the holospaces system emulator and presents it as a full screen terminal. It is not a simulation: the boot messages are the kernel's own, and the shell is real Debian with real . The holospace is the virtual machine. There is no nested QEMU and no KVM see Constraints . How it maps to the architecture (conformance) Piece What it is Spec / ADR · witness The CPU a real AArch64 (+RV64GC) machine compiled to Wasm, bound to the hologram host ABI ADR 009 · CC 9, CC 35/36/37 The boot real Linux 6.6 mounts an rootfs over ; SBI/PSCI hand off ADR 011 · CC 14, CC 36 The rootfs a Debian base OCI image ingested by κ → κ disk (CC 7) ADR 011 · CC 10 → CC 7 The terminal the real xterm.js over the holospace's console channel ADR 010 · CC 11 Networking + userspace TCP/IP NAT, tunnelled to a WebSocket egress relay (so / reach out) ADR 014 · CC 16 The ISA AArch64, so stock binaries run with no per ISA workaround ADR 021 · CC 37 Identity the OS image is content (a κ), not a registry reference; verified by re derivation Laws L1/L5 The terminal is the full official xterm.js 5.5.0 package the core plus the addons that make it a complete browser terminal: WebGL (GPU rendering), web links , search , unicode11 (correct wide/emoji widths), and clipboard (OSC 52). All are pinned UMD builds in , verified by sha256 ( ) external authorities, not loaded from a CDN (Law L5; ADR 010). Core + fit match the engine's CC 13 pin; the rest are the official packages for 5.5.0. Copy/paste (Ctrl+Shift+C/V) and find (Ctrl+Shift+F) are wired to xterm's own APIs. Files the holospace: xterm.js terminal + thin chrome (name · κ · status), COI shim, the boot overlay, wired to the worker. the VM worker: fetches the artifacts, assembles the rootfs ( ), boots , pumps in adaptive chunks, streams to xterm and feeds keystrokes back with . Warm resumes from a κ snapshot (CC 30) when one is present (OPFS or shipped). the artifact recipe (runs in CI / the devcontainer, not on Windows): the arm64 kernel + the Debian rootfs OCI layer. the full pinned xterm.js 5.5.0 package (core + webgl/fit/ web links/search/unicode11/clipboard/serialize) + css + . the pinned 1.0.3 package (braille motion frames); drives the live boot animation rendered inside the terminal. Pick a style with (dna · braille · helix · pulse · sparkle · …). Build the artifacts (CI / devcontainer) Requires , , (and, with , the toolchain). The kernel defaults to the already proven CC 37 arm64 image (cgroups/namespaces/overlayfs + virtio + no initramfs a Debian capable CLI kernel). Run Without the artifacts the holospace loads, shows the terminal chrome, and reports "OS image not built yet" with the build command the honest state. Constraints (read before expecting KVM) No KVM. The emulator core implements RV64GC/Zicsr and AArch64 EL0/EL1 only there is no RISC V H extension, no ARM EL2, no x86 VMX. There is no hardware virtualisation to expose to the guest and no . This is architectural. QEMU only in TCG. You can in the guest and run it, but only in software (TCG) mode emulation on emulation, slow. The intended model is the opposite: the holospace itself is your VM (a real kernel on a real device tree), so you rarely want a second emulator inside it. Performance. A pure interpreter CPU in a tab is far below bare metal. It is a real Debian box for building, scripting, and tooling not a benchmark host.
